checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 balance sheet / 10-Q FY2014 balance sheet AR line

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end 2011-12-31 = 33576836.38; end 2012-12-31 = 35928550.94, while Revenues 2011=155872736.58 fell to Revenues 2012=149118089.77
evidence.location: 10-K FY2011/10-K FY2012 balance sheet and income statement

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill end 2011-12-31 = 653673811.86; end 2012-12-31 = 745766616.73 (14.1% growth) while Revenues fell from 155872736.58 (2011) to 149118089.77 (2012)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2011/FY2012 balance sheet intangibles line and income statement Revenues

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill of 853251351.23 (2014-12-31) is approximately 4.4x annual Revenues of 194392809.53 (2014), indicating a heavily acquisition/intangible-laden balance sheet relative to operating scale
evidence.location: 10-Q Q4 2014 balance sheet / FY2014 10-K income statement

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2012 (restated) income statement and cash flow statement
10-K FY2014 income statement and cash flow statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or CostOfGoodsSold concept is present in the provided financial_series_point_in_time data set
evidence.location: Data coverage review - series list contains no inventory or COGS tag

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss 2014: Q1=25189475.92, Q2=14885341.65, Q3=14236302.15, Q4=10060112.27 (high variability) vs Revenues Q1=48946850.39, Q2=50129029.48, Q3=47940417.71 (comparatively stable)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2009 balance sheet and 10-Q Q4 2014 balance sheet

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filings recorded in 2010-04-30, 2011-04-29, 2012-04-27, 2013-04-30, 2014-04-30, and 2015-04-30 - an annual amendment pattern for six consecutive years

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans annual and quarterly points from 2007/2008 through Q1 2015 for Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingIncomeLoss, Assets, Liabilities, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, LongTermDebt and cash flow statement line items, providing multi-year trend coverage; however inventory, allowance for doubtful accounts, and cost-of-goods-sold concepts are absent, limiting margin/inventory analysis

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature/aggressive revenue recognition or extended customer payment terms causing receivables to build up faster than sales growth, which could mean revenue is recognized ahead of collectible cash or bad-debt allowances are insufficient

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end 2013-12-31 = 38258349.87 rising to 50057803.71 at 2014-12-31 (+30.9%) while Revenues grew only from 182332054.07 (2013) to 194392809.53 (2014) (+6.6%)
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew 6.9% (33576836.38 to 35928550.94) between 2011-12-31 and 2012-12-31 while Revenues declined 4.3% (155872736.58 to 149118089.77) over the same period

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Large debt-funded acquisitions have built an intangible asset base (~853M at 2014-12-31) that is roughly 4.4x annual revenue (194M); if underlying acquired royalty/IP cash flows underperform, impairment charges may be delayed or understated, overstating carrying value of intangibles and net income

rationale_evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill rose from 528793975.83 (2009-12-31) to 853251351.23 (2014-12-31) and further to 919911501.09 (2015-03-31), funded in part by LongTermDebt increasing from 246292368.19 (2010-12-31) to 626412467.0 (2015-03-31)
IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill grew 14.1% (653673811.86 to 745766616.73) in 2012 even as Revenues declined 4.3% (155872736.58 to 149118089.77) in the same year

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible misclassification or technical covenant/maturity reclassification of long-term debt to current liabilities in a single quarter, later reversed, which could reflect either an accounting error, a covenant breach quickly cured, or aggressive current/non-current debt classification management

rationale_evidence.quote: LiabilitiesCurrent spiked to 308530198.91 at 2014-06-30 versus 57340532.66 at 2014-03-31 and 60102743.63 at 2014-09-30; correspondingly LongTermDebtCurrent jumped to 280734450.09 at 2014-06-30 versus 25814071.07 in the surrounding quarters

top_signals: Accounts receivable grew far faster than revenue in 2013-2014 (+30.9% vs +6.6%) and grew even as revenue declined in 2012, raising revenue-recognition/collectibility concerns

Company filed a 10-K/A amendment every year from 2010 through 2015 combined with dense recurring SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence in 2013-2015, suggesting unresolved accounting/disclosure issues under SEC review
Balance sheet is dominated by debt-funded intangible assets (~853M, ~4.4x revenue) built through acquisitions, creating material impairment risk if royalty/IP cash flows underperform
One-quarter anomalous spike in current liabilities/current portion of long-term debt (2014-06-30) that reversed the following quarter, indicating possible debt classification irregularity
